text: Concurrent training sequence (strength-first vs endurance-first) does not affect VO2max improvements.
Performing strength training before endurance training produces greater lower limb strength gains than the reverse order.
The strength-first advantage for lower limb strength is more pronounced in elderly populations and in women.
The strength-before-endurance advantage for lower limb strength emerges with training periods longer than 8 weeks and training frequency of twice per week.
Strength-first concurrent training improves maximum knee flexion and knee extension strength more than endurance-first, but does not differ for lower limb power.
specific_value: knee flexion p = 0.040, knee extension p = 0.026, power p = 0.523

text: When combining strength and endurance in the same session, runners prioritizing strength gains should perform strength exercises first, while those prioritizing aerobic fitness can use either order.
